role_title: Sr. Staff Technical Program Manager (Hardware) - Dot

location.office_time_required: minimum two days per week onsite

seniority.level: Sr. Staff IC (very senior individual contributor)
level_signals: 8+ years of industry experience working in engineering and program management
Title includes 'Sr. Staff' signaling a top-of-IC-ladder designation
Comp range $193,800–$285,000 base consistent with Sr. Staff at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: B.S., M.S., or PhD. in engineering, e.g. robotics, mechanical, electrical or computer science
8+ years of industry experience working in engineering and program management
Experience with software and hardware development processes
Experience taking hardware from product requirements to mass production overseas, e.g. consumer or automotive electronics
A track record of managing complex, cross-functional programs
Excellent understanding of vehicle mechanicals and electronics and their interplay with firmware and software for a robotics system
Strong knowledge of autonomous vehicle sensor systems and compute systems
Working knowledge of program management tools, such as Jira, Confluence, Smartsheet/MS Project, Google Workplace applications

scope.surface: Autonomous delivery robots (Dot) — hardware development, testing, validation, and manufacturing for current and next-generation robotics systems
scope.business_model: Internal R&D / product development within DoorDash Labs; delivery automation as part of DoorDash's last-mile logistics platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Hardware milestone delivery (concept to public roads)
Manufacturing readiness and mass production timelines
Cross-functional issue resolution and program health
core_responsibilities: Program manage and help lead development, testing, validation and manufacturing of current and next generation mechanical, electrical and electronic robotics hardware
Take hardware projects from product requirements or engineering concept to public roads driving
Work with leaders and engineers across Hardware, Software, Firmware, Controls, Autonomy and Ops to plan, track, follow up and resolve issues
Leverage expertise not only in hardware but software and firmware integration as well
Manage vendor relationships domestically and internationally, both for prototyping and mass production

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($193,800–$285,000 base)
Clear product surface: autonomous delivery robot (Dot) with well-defined scope
Reports to Lead of TPM team — clear organizational placement
Role is embedded in DoorDash Labs, an independent team with autonomy to build from the ground-up
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
red_flags: Hybrid requirement: minimum two days per week onsite in San Francisco or Oakland — eliminates remote candidates
No 'nice to have' section means all requirements are treated as hard — potentially narrow candidate pool
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international applicants
Pay range is very wide ($193,800–$285,000), which may indicate significant variability in level or location-based adjustment without full transparency
